chapters.summary: This chapter covers electrostatic potential due to a point charge, dipole, system of charges, equipotential surfaces, electrical potential energy, capacitors and capacitance, combination of capacitors, and dielectrics.

keyConcepts.title: Equipotential Surface
keyConcepts.description: A surface which has the same electrostatic potential at every point. No work is done in moving a charge on an equipotential surface.

keyConcepts.title: Dielectrics and Polarization
keyConcepts.description: Non-conducting substances that develop an electric dipole moment in the presence of an external electric field, reducing the net field within.

formulas.name: Electrostatic Potential (V)
formulas.description: Potential at distance r due to point charge q.

formulas.name: Capacitance of Parallel Plate Capacitor
formulas.description: A is plate area, d is plate separation, K is dielectric constant.

videos.title: Electrostatic Potential & Capacitance Class 12 One Shot

quizzes.question: If a dielectric slab of dielectric constant K is inserted between the plates of a parallel plate capacitor, its capacitance becomes:
quizzes.explanation: Inserting a dielectric of constant K increases the capacitance by a factor of K (C' = K * C) due to polarization within the dielectric.

chapters.summary: Covers rate of reaction, factors affecting rate (concentration, temperature, catalyst), order and molecularity, integrated rate equations for zero and first order, half-life, and collision theory.

keyConcepts.title: Order of Reaction
keyConcepts.description: The sum of powers of the concentration of reactants in the rate law expression. It can be zero, fractional, or an integer.

keyConcepts.title: Arrhenius Equation
keyConcepts.description: Describes the temperature dependence of reaction rates: k = A * exp(-Ea / RT), where Ea is activation energy.

formulas.name: First-Order Integrated Rate Equation
formulas.description: [A]0 is initial concentration, [A]t is concentration at time t.

videos.title: Chemical Kinetics Class 12 Full Chapter Revision

quizzes.question: The rate constant of a reaction depends on:
quizzes.explanation: According to the Arrhenius equation, the rate constant (k) depends exponentially on the absolute temperature (T).
